article_title: Databricks Lakebase Reaches General Availability on Azure as Serverless PostgreSQL Expands to 14 Regions

content_preview.title: Databricks Lakebase Reaches General Availability on Azure as Serverless PostgreSQL Expands to 14 Regions
content_preview.summary: Databricks has made Lakebase, its serverless PostgreSQL database built on acquired Neon technology, generally available on Azure with autoscaling compute, high availability, and compliance certifications across 14 regions.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
Databricks has announced the general availability of Lakebase on Azure, marking the second major cloud platform to receive production-ready support for the company's serverless PostgreSQL database. The [March 2, 2026 release](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/databricks/release-notes/product/2026/march) extends Lakebase to 14 Azure regions worldwide, up from just three regions during the public preview period.

Lakebase is a serverless, PostgreSQL-based online transaction processing (OLTP)...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Thorough product news with excellent technical detail on regions, features, acquisitions, and March updates timeline. Well-structured for developer audience.
editor_notes.source_verification: Microsoft Learn (fetched): Confirms Lakebase GA on Azure March 2, 11 new regions (exact list matches), autoscaling default, HA, compliance (HIPAA/C5/TISAX), Lakehouse Sync beta March 12, OAuth roles, budget policies, Lakebase in Apps March 16, Private Link preview March 9. InfoQ (fetched): Confirms Mooncake Labs October 2025 acquisition, Zaharia quotes on branching/snapshots/AI agents, PostgreSQL 17, pgvector, ephemeral compute architecture. SiliconAngle (fetched): Confirms Neon ~$1B acquisition May 2025, 10,000+ QPS, 35-day recovery, pgvector/PostGIS, Unity Catalog integration.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified. Region list matches exactly. Acquisition details, dates, technical specs all confirmed. learn.microsoft.com is Microsoft's official documentation — highly authoritative despite not being on allowlist.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. No promotional language despite covering a product launch.
editor_notes.originality: No prior coverage of Databricks Lakebase. Unique topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Accurate, well-sourced database infrastructure news. Ready for publication.
